description_long: This guard stone consists of a roughly hewn, upright stone block mounted atop a rectangular stone pedestal, both set on a square stone base. The upper stone is irregular in shape, with a weathered, natural surface, while the lower pedestal is more geometric and squared. Moss and lichen growth are visible at the junction between the two stones and along the base, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure. One face of the pedestal features faint, carved geometric patterns or symbols, suggesting historical or decorative significance. The stone surfaces are mottled gray with patches of green and brown, and the base is surrounded by earth and debris. The overall appearance is robust and ancient, with clear signs of age and environmental wear.
description: A weathered, upright stone block atop a rectangular pedestal with moss and faint carvings, set on a square stone base. The surfaces are rough, gray, and show signs of age and outdoor exposure.
description_view_0: Front view shows the irregular upper stone and squared pedestal, with moss at the junction and a flat, weathered face.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the uneven, jagged profile of the upper stone and the rectangular base, with visible moss along the edge.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals faint geometric carvings or symbols on the pedestal, with more pronounced moss and discoloration at the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the angled upper stone and a relatively plain pedestal face, with moss and earth at the base.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the edge of urban streets, near driveways, building corners, or historical sites to protect structures from vehicle impact.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright with the base resting on or slightly embedded in the ground surface.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible surface erosion, chipping at edges and corners, discoloration from moss and lichen, and smoothing of high-contact areas. Most wear is likely due to weathering, occasional vehicle contact, and prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_historical_markings: Faint geometric carvings or symbols are present on one face of the pedestal, possibly of historical or decorative origin.
